query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "memory is reconstructed, not retrieved: graph memory for llm agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) by investigating the same core mechanism (LLM agent memory write-retrieve-act pipelines) and variables (memory integrity, retrieval authority, and poisoning vulnerabilities) central to the user's question about graph-based memory reconstruction.
